title: Parent Graphs And Polynomials - Quiz
description: Test your understanding of parent functions, transformations, polynomial degree, leading coefficient, end behavior, and polynomial roots

questions.question: What is the parent function of all quadratic functions?
questions.explanation: f(x) = x² is the parent function for all quadratic functions. Its graph is a parabola opening upward with vertex at the origin. All other quadratics are transformations of this parent function.

questions.question: What transformation does f(x) = (x - 3)² represent compared to f(x) = x²?
questions.explanation: Replacing x with (x - h) shifts the graph h units to the right. Since h = 3, the graph of x² shifts 3 units to the right. Note: (x - 3) means right, while (x + 3) means left — it's counterintuitive.

questions.question: What is the degree of the polynomial f(x) = 4x⁵ - 3x³ + 2x - 7?
questions.explanation: The degree of a polynomial is the highest power of the variable. The term 4x⁵ has the highest exponent (5), so the polynomial has degree 5.

questions.question: The leading coefficient of f(x) = -2x⁴ + 5x³ - x + 3 is:
questions.explanation: The leading coefficient is the coefficient of the term with the highest degree. The highest-degree term is -2x⁴, so the leading coefficient is -2.

questions.question: For a polynomial with odd degree and positive leading coefficient, what is the end behavior?

options.text: Left end points down, right end points up
Left end points up, right end points down

questions.explanation: For odd-degree polynomials with a positive leading coefficient, as x → -∞, f(x) → -∞ (down to the left), and as x → +∞, f(x) → +∞ (up to the right). Think of f(x) = x³ as the simplest example.

questions.question: How many roots (counting multiplicity) does a polynomial of degree n have?

options.text: Exactly n roots (by the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra)

questions.explanation: The Fundamental Theorem of Algebra states that a polynomial of degree n has exactly n roots when counted with multiplicity (including complex roots). For example, x² + 1 has two complex roots: i and -i.

questions.question: What transformation does f(x) = -x² represent?
questions.explanation: Multiplying f(x) by -1 reflects the graph over the x-axis. The parabola f(x) = x² opens upward, while f(x) = -x² opens downward — it's a mirror image across the x-axis.

questions.question: What are the roots of the polynomial f(x) = x² - 5x + 6?
questions.explanation: Factor: x² - 5x + 6 = (x - 2)(x - 3). Setting each factor to zero: x - 2 = 0 gives x = 2, and x - 3 = 0 gives x = 3.

questions.question: Which parent function has a graph that looks like a 'V' shape?
questions.explanation: The absolute value function f(x) = |x| creates a V-shaped graph with its vertex at the origin. It equals x when x ≥ 0 and -x when x < 0.

questions.question: The graph of f(x) = 2x² compared to f(x) = x² is:
questions.explanation: Multiplying by a coefficient |a| > 1 causes a vertical stretch, making the parabola narrower. f(x) = 2x² grows twice as fast as x², so the parabola is narrower. If 0 < |a| < 1, the graph would be wider.
